$meta.source: Yick Hoe Group of Company – Structural Steel Handbook
philosophy: One collection per product family — not one universal 'products' collection.
Flat documents: dimensions, section properties, and mechanical properties are embedded fields, not sub-collections. They are always read together.
Sub-collections are used ONLY for safe_load tables because they are large (up to 13 span entries × multiple grades) and often queried independently.
The _meta block is lean: created_at, updated_at, active, handbook_page. Nothing else.
All numeric values stored in SI base units (mm, cm, cm², cm³, cm⁴, kg/m, kN, MPa). Imperial fields suffixed _in or _lb.
Every document has a 'keywords' string array for array-contains-any full-text workaround in Firestore.
Composite indexes are declared for every cross-field query pattern.

structural_beams._description: I-Beams, Universal Beams, Universal Columns, Light Beams, Joists. One document per section size + mass per metre.

dimensions._note: Embedded — always read with the document.

section_properties._note: Embedded — structural engineers always need this alongside dimensions.

spans._note: Keyed by span in metres as a string ('2.0', '2.5' …). Map is faster than array for point lookups by span.

flag_legend.BOLD: Web buckling governs — stiffener may be needed
flag_legend.SHEAR_CONTROLLED: Load limited by web shear, not bending

piles._description: Bearing piles and sheet steel piles. Separate from structural_beams because dimension schema differs (equal flange & web thickness).

hollow_sections._description: Cold-formed and hot-formed SHS, RHS, CHS. Shape and formation are the key filter axes.

height_mm.description: Height B for RHS (null for SHS/CHS)

pipes._description: API ERW pipes, seamless pipes, standard BS/JIS/ASTM pipes. OD and wall thickness are the primary search axes.

channels._description: Plain channels, lipped (C) channels, DIN channels, U-channels, inch-series channels.

lip_depth_mm.description: C dimension for lipped channels; null for plain

Zx_reduced_cm3.description: Reduced section modulus Zx1 for lipped channels

angles._description: Equal and unequal angles (mild steel and stainless steel).

leg_b_mm.description: Equal to leg_a_mm for equal angles

bars._description: Flat, round, square, hex bars — mild steel, stainless steel, carbon steel, alloy steel, cold-finished.

mechanical_properties._note: Embedded because bars are almost always selected by grade+mechanical spec together.

chemical_composition._note: Embedded for alloy/carbon steel bars — composition is part of the selection criteria.

plates._description: Structural plates, chequer plates, cold-rolled coils/sheets, galvanised sheets, all plate products.

pipe_fittings._description: Wrought steel butt-welding fittings (mild steel and stainless). One doc per fitting type + NB + schedule + material.

flanges._description: All flange types, both mild steel and stainless steel.

stainless_corrosion_resistance._description: Chemical corrosion resistance ratings for SS 304 and 316. One doc per substance+concentration+condition.

_rating_scale._suffix_1: Subject to pitting at air line or when allowed to dry

misc_products._description: Galvanised serrated gratings and expanded metal. Small catalogue, single collection.

mesh_size.description: For expanded metal: SWO x SWD mm

reference_tables._description: Read-only engineering reference data: BS449 Table 3a (bending stress), Table 17a (axial compression), deflection coefficients, high-strength steel grades. One document per table.

BS449-TABLE3A-GRADE43.table_name: Allowable bending stress pbc (N/mm²) for Grade 43 steel
BS449-TABLE3A-GRADE43._note: Keyed map: outer key = l_over_ry (string), inner key = D_over_T (string). Faster than array for two-axis lookup.

BS449-TABLE17A-GRADE43.table_name: Allowable axial compression stress pc (N/mm²) for Grade 43 steel
BS449-TABLE17A-GRADE43._note: Outer key = l_over_r tens digit (string), inner keys 0–9 are the units digit. Matches Table 17a layout exactly.

HIGH_STRENGTH_STEEL_GRADES.table_name: High strength steel grade equivalents and mechanical properties
HIGH_STRENGTH_STEEL_GRADES._note: Array of grade objects. Queried as a whole document; no sub-field indexing needed.

indexing_strategy._note: Single-field indexes are auto-created by Firestore. Only composite indexes are listed here.
